title: National Fisheries Development Board building
tagline: National Fisheries Development Board building — heritage city of Telangana

heroImage.alt: National Fisheries Development Board building, Telangana

overview.short: The National Fisheries Development Board building, located in Hyderabad, India, is the headquarters of the eponymous National Fisheries Development Board.
overview.description: The National Fisheries Development Board building, located in Hyderabad, India, is the headquarters of the eponymous National Fisheries Development Board. The building is notable as a unique example of mimetic or novelty architecture, being designed in the shape of a gigantic fish. The Building was designed and conceptualized by Central Public Works Department (CPWD) . The four-storey building was officially opened in April 2012, and was reportedly inspired by Frank Gehry's monumental ‘Fish’ sculpture in Barcelona which was completed in 1992.

topPlaces.name: Mir Alam Tank
topPlaces.description: Mir Alam Tank is a reservoir in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It is located to the south of Musi river.

topPlaces.name: Solitaire Global Schools
topPlaces.description: Solitaire Global Schools (commonly abbreviated to SGS) is a group of schools based in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It was established in June 2017 and has two branches in Hyderabad at Attapur and Kattedan.

topPlaces.name: Kismatpur
topPlaces.description: Kismatpur is a census town in Ranga Reddy district, Telangana, India. It falls under Gandipet Mandal.

topPlaces.name: Rukn-ud-Daula lake
topPlaces.description: Rukn-ud-Daula lake or Bum-Rukn-ud-Dowla is a historic lake situated in Shivarampally village in Hyderabad, India.

topPlaces.name: Kings Colony, Shastripuram
topPlaces.description: Shastripuram Housing Colony is an area in the Ranga Reddy District of Telangana, India. It comes under Rajendra Nagar Circle 6 Government of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Nehru Zoological Park
topPlaces.description: Nehru Zoological Park (also known as Zoo Park) is a zoo located near Mir Alam Tank in Bahadurpura, Telangana, India. It is one of the most visited destinations in Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Rajendranagar mandal
topPlaces.description: Rajendranagar is a mandal in Ranga Reddy district of the Indian state of Telangana. and it is also Rajendranagar revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Bandlaguda Jagir
topPlaces.description: Bandlaguda Jagir is a satellite city of Hyderabad and a municipal corporation in Rangareddy district, Telangana, India. It Falls Under Gandipet mandal of Rajendranagar revenue division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit National Fisheries Development Board building?
faq.a: The best time to visit National Fisheries Development Board building is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach National Fisheries Development Board building?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for National Fisheries Development Board building?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of National Fisheries Development Board building at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in National Fisheries Development Board building?
faq.a: Stays in National Fisheries Development Board building start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in National Fisheries Development Board building?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mir Alam Tank, Solitaire Global Schools, Kismatpur, Rukn-ud-Daula lake, Kings Colony, Shastripuram.
